fields.definition_origin: Coined informally in product-design discourse 2022-2024 (Headway/Headspace/Cal-AI teardowns on Mobbin and Growth.Design); the term 'felt personalization' versus 'data-collected personalization' was popularized in the Cal AI breakdown thread (Cesar Alvarez, X/Twitter, 2024) and the Mau Baron Starter Story playbook (2025). The technique itself goes back to direct-response 'merge tags' (mailmerge era), reapplied to mobile onboarding state.
fields.core_mechanism: Each questionnaire answer mutates a shared client/server context object that is referenced verbatim in later screen copy, illustration, and metric reveals. The user reads their own answer back as proof that the system 'heard' them — which compounds endowment (IKEA Effect, Norton et al. 2012) and sunk cost (Arkes & Blumer 1985) because the path now feels uniquely theirs. It also exploits the self-reference effect (Rogers, Kuiper & Kirker 1977) — copy mentioning the user's stated goal is encoded more deeply than generic copy.
fields.neuroscience_correlate: Self-reference effect engages medial prefrontal cortex (Kelley et al. 2002, J Cog Neurosci) — copy referencing 'you' + a previously-stated value about self produces deeper encoding and stronger commitment than generic copy. [uncertain on direct ERP measurement during mobile onboarding]
fields.empirical_evidence: (1) Mobbin teardowns of Cal AI (April 2024 capture, 33 steps) explicitly show step 7 'we'll create a plan to lose [user's stated weight goal] by [user's stated date]' threading from step 4. (2) Headway/Headspace teardown (Tear Them Down, Medium 2023) documents stitched copy at the goal-confirmation screen referencing the prior 'biggest blocker' answer. (3) Growth.Design case study #163 (Noom 2022) measured a 14% lift in trial start when the AI plan reveal stitched five prior answers vs. a generic plan; sample size not disclosed but cited as 'multi-week split test'. (4) Flo period-tracker (TheAppFuel teardown 2024) stitches cycle-irregularity flag from screen 4 into the symptom-tracker explainer at screen 11. (5) Mau Baron Prayer Lock (Starter Story 2025): stitching the user's stated 'biggest spiritual struggle' into the AI-generated prayer reveal is the load-bearing trick — Baron credits stitched personalization with tripling organic conversion alongside the 5→15 min extension.
fields.measurable_outcome_metric: Step-7→step-N completion rate, AI-plan-reveal screen exit rate, paywall trial-start conversion, and qualitative 'this app gets me' NPS / review sentiment. Secondary: percentage of reveal-screen copy that contains user-supplied tokens (target: ≥3 stitched references at the climax).
fields.conversion_lift_benchmark: +10-15% trial start at the AI-reveal paywall vs unstitched control (Growth.Design Noom case study, single test) — broader benchmark unverified. Mau Baron quotes 3× organic conversion lift but conflates stitching with the 5→15 min extension.
fields.engineering_tactics: (1) State machine context object (XState v5 actor context, Zustand store, or Redux slice) that accumulates `{ goal, painpoint, identity, metrics, archetype, locale }` keys as steps complete. (2) Every screen template uses interpolation tokens (`{{user.goal}}`, `{{user.target_date}}`) resolved at render time. (3) Server mirror — every step posts to `/api/onboarding/state` so the AI plan reveal endpoint can re-fetch the canonical context for LLM prompt construction (avoid trusting client-only state at the climax). (4) Fallback copy — for every stitched string, define a default for users who skipped the source step. (5) Localization — stitched tokens must be grammatically safe across locales (gendered languages need fallback variants; numbers need locale-aware formatting). (6) Telemetry — emit `personalization_token_stitched` with token name + screen id so funnel analysis can prove the stitch was actually displayed (not just defined).
fields.real_world_examples: (1) Cal AI step 30 reveal: 'To reach [stated weight goal] by [stated date], you'll need [computed daily calorie target]' — three tokens stitched from steps 4, 6, 18. (2) Headspace 2024 onboarding step 8 'You said the hardest part of your day is [evening/morning] — let's start there' — single stitch from step 5. (3) Flo step 11 cycle-tracker explainer 'Because your cycles are irregular, we'll watch for [specific symptom set]' — stitched from screen 4 irregularity flag plus screen 9 symptom checklist. (4) Noom (2022 teardown) step 22 'Most people who said [your biggest blocker] succeed when they [tactic]' — explicit social-proof stitch. (5) Prayer Lock (Mau Baron) AI-generated personalized prayer at step ~12 references the user's stated 'biggest struggle' and 'who they're praying for' — stitched verbatim into the LLM prompt. (6) Headway book-summary onboarding step 9 'Based on your goal of [career growth], we recommend starting with [Atomic Habits]' — stitched goal-to-content recommendation.
fields.mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile-primary; the technique works identically on web (Typeform's hidden-fields pattern is the web canonical), but mobile carries higher abandonment cost so stitching pays off more. On mobile, persist the context object to MMKV/AsyncStorage every step so termination/backgrounding never drops the stitch chain.
fields.platform_design_language_alignment: Neutral. Stitched personalization is copy-system, not chrome — works equally well under Liquid Glass, Material 3 Expressive, or fully custom design languages. Liquid Glass's bolder left-aligned alert typography (WWDC25) makes stitched headlines particularly impactful since the user's own words land at the visual peak of the screen.
fields.category_fit: Strongest fit: wellness/habit (Cal AI, Flo, Noom, Headspace), AI-wrapper (Cal AI, Prayer Lock), edtech (Headway, Blinkist), fitness, dating-profile-builders, journaling. Weak fit: utilities, single-feature tools, B2B productivity (Linear, Notion don't stitch — their value prop is universal).
fields.tooling_options: XState v5 (state machine + persistence), Zustand + persist middleware (lightweight), MMKV (synchronous RN storage), Supabase/Firebase for server mirror, OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini APIs for AI reveal that consumes the context, Plotline's onboarding builder supports variable interpolation, Adapty offers 'placement attributes' for paywall stitching, Superwall's audience filters can stitch into paywall copy.
fields.ai_dependency: optional: stitching itself is just string interpolation; the AI plan reveal that consumes the stitched context (item 12) is where LLM dependency lives. Small classifier models (e.g., GPT-4o-mini, Claude Haiku) suffice — stitched tokens reduce hallucination because the LLM is constrained by literal user inputs.
fields.claude_design_application: Instruct Claude Design to define the context schema first, then template every onboarding screen against tokens, never hardcoded copy. Prompt fragment: 'Define a TypeScript type `OnboardingContext = { goal: string; painpoint: string; targetDate: Date; identity: 'runner' | 'walker' | 'beginner'; ... }`. For each of the 12 onboarding screens, output a `<Screen>` component whose copy uses `{ctx.goal}` / `{ctx.painpoint}` interpolation. The reveal screen at index 10 must reference at least 3 prior tokens by name in the headline. Forbid: hardcoded "your goal" — must read user input back verbatim. Provide a fallback for every token in case the user skipped a screen.' Reference v2 emotional-design 03a output format for archetype-tinted copy register.
fields.stitch_application: Stitch can't run code, so describe the stitch points as copy variations. Prompt fragment: 'Mockup a sequence of 5 onboarding screens. Screen 1 asks the user their primary goal (multiple-choice: "Lose weight / Build muscle / Eat better / Sleep better"). Screen 2 asks target timeframe. Screen 3 asks biggest blocker. Screen 4 (loader) shows "Building your plan…". Screen 5 (reveal) headline reads VERBATIM: "Here's how you'll [user's goal] by [user's date], even with [user's blocker]." Show three variants of screen 5 with different example user inputs filled in to prove the stitching works. Surface tokens visually in a different color (e.g., #5E6AD2) so reviewers can see what is dynamic.'
fields.ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI codegen typically generates each screen as an independent component with hardcoded copy ('Welcome! Let's set your goal') instead of templating against a shared context. It also forgets to persist context across navigations and drops state on background termination. Counter-prompt: 'Output the OnboardingContext type FIRST. Every screen must import this context and interpolate at least one field into its copy. Persist context to MMKV on every transition. Provide a fallback string for every interpolation in case the field is undefined. Forbid: hardcoded user-facing strings that should reference the user's prior answers.'
fields.anti_patterns: (1) Stitching that exposes raw machine state ('You selected option_3') instead of human re-readable values — destroys the 'app gets me' feeling. (2) Stitched copy that's grammatically broken in non-English locales (gender, plurals, declension). (3) Stitching the user's name in every sentence (uncanny-valley creepy — Headspace 2018 had to dial this back). (4) Personalization that contradicts the user's prior answer ('we recommend [generic option]' when user already specified preference). (5) Pairs against the 'feel-fake personalization' antipattern where copy says 'personalized for you' but no user input is referenced.
fields.regulatory_risk: Low: stitching uses data the user voluntarily provided in the same session for the explicit purpose of personalization — generally aligned with GDPR Article 6(1)(a) consent and Article 5(1)(b) purpose limitation. Risk escalates to Medium if stitched tokens include health/biometric data persisted to server analytics without explicit consent (GDPR Article 9 special category data) or if the stitch is used for cross-session targeting beyond stated purpose.
fields.platform_constraints_2026: No platform restriction. Apple Guideline 5.1.1(v) data minimization applies to the underlying questions, not the stitching itself — stitching maximizes the value extracted from the minimum data collected, which is actually a compliance positive.
fields.accessibility_implications: VoiceOver/TalkBack must read the resolved (stitched) text, not the template — straightforward when using standard React Native Text components but failure mode if developers concatenate strings unsafely. Stitched values that include numbers should use accessibilityLabel with spelled-out units ('150 pounds' not '150 lb'). Dynamic-type / text-scaling — stitched headlines can overflow if user input is long; constrain via numberOfLines or shrink-to-fit.
fields.pitfalls_caveats: Latency at the AI-reveal climax — server-side stitching adds 200-500ms; pre-fetch as soon as the user enters the loader screen. Offline: cache the context locally so reveal still works without network (use a local fallback message). Stitching only works if questions are good — bad data in, bad stitch out. Avoid stitching free-text fields without sanitization (XSS / injection risk if rendered as HTML).
fields.cross_cultural_validity: English-first technique. CJK locales: stitched names in honorific languages (JP -san, KR -nim) need locale-aware suffixes. Gendered languages (ES, FR, DE, AR) require gender flag from the user or gender-neutral fallback templates. RTL markets: token order in templates must use named placeholders (`{goal}`) not positional (`%1$s`) so translators can reorder. Numeric formats must localize (1,000 vs 1.000). Mau Baron's Prayer Lock localized the AI-generated prayer reveal across 8 locales by stitching only against locale-specific prompt templates.
fields.prescribed_action: Define a single OnboardingContext object that accumulates every user answer; every subsequent screen must interpolate at least one prior answer into its copy by name (the user's goal, painpoint, or identity word read back verbatim). At the climax (AI-reveal or paywall headline) stitch ≥3 prior tokens to make the path feel uniquely theirs. Never write hardcoded copy for screens after step 3.
fields.questionnaire_relevance: Each question's answer becomes a stitch source — write questions whose answers are short, displayable strings ("lose 10 lb", "sleep better", "runner") rather than coded enums. Phrase questions so the answer reads naturally back in second-person copy.
fields.aha_relevance: Stitching IS what makes the AI-reveal feel earned rather than canned — the user sees their own words inside the personalized plan and the Aha lands as 'this was made for me' rather than 'this is a template'. Without stitching the reveal feels generic and Aha collapses.
fields.emotional_archetype_alignment: Caregiver (gentle echoing of the user's pain), Magician (transformation framed in the user's words), Sage (data-driven personal plan), Lover (intimate 'I see you' tone). Mismatch with Outlaw (rule-breaking — user wants to feel anonymous, stitching is too cozy), Ruler (formal — first-name stitching feels chummy in formal categories like fintech).
fields.anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against 'Feel-Fake Personalization' — interfaces that claim 'personalized for you!' but display generic plans. Stitching is the proof-of-personalization that defeats the antipattern.
sources: [Self-reference effect in memory — Rogers, Kuiper & Kirker (1977)](https://psycnet.apa.org/record/1978-00421-001)
